question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcProperties object is a java.util.Properties (which is a Hashtable and thread-unsafe) to perform a concurrent modification attack in a multi-threaded startup scenario, injecting malicious properties that cause the JDBC driver to connect to a rogue database with reordered transaction history?
